issues.detail: CWAN completed its $8.4B take-private (Permira/Warburg Pincus/Francisco Partners/Temasek) and delisted from NYSE on June 25, 2026. It still trades a frozen last-print of $24.55-24.56 in the data drop — that flat, unchanging quote is itself the tradability signal (no live market). portfolios.json growth sleeve still lists CWAN. Unresolved for a third consecutive run (first flagged 2026-06-30).
issues.action: Remove CWAN from data/portfolios.json growth sleeve.

roster_integrity.universe_note: ETHA and IBIT (Digital-Asset-ETF sleeve) appear in the data drop (52 holdings total) but are absent from portfolios.json (50 tickers). portfolios.json covers 50 of 52 priced holdings. universe_incomplete=true until corrected. CTRA→DVN mapping from the prior known issue has been fixed in portfolios.json (DVN now listed directly).

summary.headline: Broad AI-infrastructure selloff on a CNBC-confirmed report that Meta may rent out excess AI compute capacity, reviving oversupply fears: LRCX -9.72%, MRVL -8.5%, TSM -6.94%, AMD -6.83% all new/persisting RED, each matching a documented AI-capex-deceleration key_risk. HUT -8.35% escalated to RED as bitcoin hit a 21-month low (Bloomberg). CRDO -4.78% persists RED on the same AI-capex thesis link. CAT -6.71% and TOL -4.39% re-emerge AMBER on tariff/rate-cyclicality themes, though the specific day's catalysts are not yet tier-1 confirmed. AEM and QCOM downgraded RED→AMBER as gold and QCOM price action stabilize. DVN's guidance-suspension key_risk is resolved (Devon issued formal 2026 production guidance post-merger). CWAN roster issue persists unresolved for a third run. TSM's July 16 earnings print (2 weeks out) added to the AI-capex calendar watch.

flags.headline: LRCX -9.72% — re-escalated to RED as broad chip-equipment selloff resumes
flags.detail: Lam Research fell 9.72% (price $391.36 vs prior close $433.49), one of several semiconductor-equipment names (with KLA, Applied Materials) that fell at least 10% in early July after a report that Meta may rent out excess AI computing capacity, reviving fears that AI processing supply is catching up to demand. This flag was downgraded RED→AMBER on 2026-06-30 after a Micron-driven recovery; today's move re-escalates it on a distinct, fresh catalyst.
flags.thesis_link: Matches LRCX key_risk #1: 'any deceleration in AI infrastructure spending or fab construction delays could trigger significant multiple compression' and key_risk #3 on WFE cyclicality reversing if AI capex moderates.

flags.headline: MRVL -8.5% — persisting RED, directly implicating Broadcom-competitive and hyperscaler-capex key_risks
flags.detail: Marvell fell 8.5% (price $271.84 vs prior close $297.59). CNBC confirms Nvidia, AMD, and Marvell all fell at least 10% intraday after the Meta excess-capacity report; the VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) fell more than 5%. This follows Marvell's Q2 rally (~200% gain) now unwinding sharply.
flags.thesis_link: Matches MRVL key_risk #3 directly: 'AI capex cycle moderation — if hyperscalers reduce data center investment due to ROI concerns... Marvell's growth trajectory... would be at risk.' The Meta capacity-rental report is precisely this ROI-concern scenario.

flags.headline: HUT -8.35% — escalated AMBER→RED as bitcoin hits a 21-month low
flags.detail: Hut 8 fell 8.35% (price $105.80 vs prior close $115.44), roughly double the -4.52% move that first triggered this flag on 2026-06-30. Bloomberg confirms bitcoin fell to a fresh 21-month low on rate-hike fears and concerns about the largest corporate BTC holder; most miners are no longer profitable on mining economics alone.
flags.thesis_link: Matches HUT key_risk #3: 'Bitcoin price exposure remains significant — average revenue per BTC mined fell 17% YoY... demonstrate the volatility of the crypto treasury strategy.' This is that risk materializing further.

flags.headline: TSM -6.94% — new RED, swept into the AI-capex selloff two weeks ahead of Q2 earnings
flags.detail: TSMC fell 6.94% (price $444.10 vs prior close $477.24) as part of the broad chip-sector reversal following the Meta excess-capacity report (CNBC). TSMC reports Q2 2026 earnings July 16, 2026 (quiet period begins July 6) — within the desk's ~2-week hyperscaler-capex calendar watch, adding near-term visibility risk on top of today's move.
flags.thesis_link: Matches TSM key_risk #2: 'AI demand cyclicality — if hyperscaler AI capex decelerates or customers pull back on orders, TSMC's $52-56B capex commitment could result in overcapacity and margin compression.'

flags.headline: AMD -6.83% — new RED on the same Meta-capacity-driven AI-oversupply fear
flags.detail: AMD fell 6.83% (price $540.89 vs prior close $580.52). CNBC names AMD explicitly among the names that fell at least 10% intraday on the Meta excess-capacity report; today's data-drop print reflects a smaller net move than the reported intraday low.
flags.thesis_link: Matches AMD key_risk #1: 'Forward P/E of 59.8x and 270% 52-week appreciation leave zero margin of safety — any AI spending deceleration or customer pause would trigger severe multiple compression.'

flags.headline: CRDO -4.78% — persisting RED, same AI-capex-dependency thesis risk
flags.detail: Credo fell 4.78% (price $259.10 vs prior close $272.10), continuing the decline flagged since June 26 (cumulative -22% from its all-time high at last check). Near-total AI/hyperscaler revenue concentration leaves no offset against the current sector-wide capacity-oversupply scare.
flags.thesis_link: Matches CRDO key_risk #3: 'AI capex cycle dependency — a slowdown in hyperscaler data center spending due to ROI concerns... would directly reduce demand for Credo's products given near-total AI exposure.'

flags.headline: CAT -6.71% — re-emerges AMBER after June 30 resolution; driver not yet tier-1 confirmed
flags.detail: Caterpillar fell 6.71% (price $991.98 vs prior close $1063.33), reversing the 2026-06-26 GREEN resolution. Non-tier-1 sources attribute the move partly to renewed tariff-cost headlines and forced passive-fund outflows from a late-June Russell index reclassification; CNBC confirms only that CAT 'pulled back almost 7% on June 30, 2026' without a specific new catalyst, and the previously reported tariff estimate ($2.2-2.4B) is unchanged from April guidance. Unconfirmed by tier-1 source as a fresh fundamental development — treating as AMBER pending confirmation.
flags.thesis_link: Touches CAT key_risk #1: 'any macro-driven order slowdown... could trigger significant multiple compression' — kept at AMBER rather than escalated given the weak/unconfirmed sourcing on the specific catalyst.

flags.headline: TOL -4.39% — new AMBER on rate-sensitivity/cyclical thesis risk
flags.detail: Toll Brothers fell 4.39% (price $157.49 vs prior close $164.72). Non-tier-1 sources report an analyst hold downgrade on valuation after a ~20% two-month run, plus a declining order backlog. Unconfirmed by tier-1 source.
flags.thesis_link: Matches TOL key_risk #1/#2: rate sensitivity and cyclical exposure with '$6.02B backlog at risk of cancellation.'

flags.headline: KEYS -4.16% — persisting AMBER, renewed decline after apparent stabilization
flags.detail: Keysight fell 4.16% (price $335.64 vs prior close $350.20), giving back the stabilization noted on 2026-06-30. Swept into the same sector-wide AI-capacity-oversupply scare.
flags.thesis_link: Matches KEYS key_risk #3: 'if hyperscaler capex slows... the current 56% order growth rate could normalize rapidly.'

flags.headline: MARA -3.74% — persisting AMBER, continuing crypto-sector weakness
flags.detail: MARA Holdings fell 3.74% (price $13.38), continuing to weigh alongside HUT as bitcoin trades at a 21-month low. MARA Holdings is down ~13% for 2026 per Bloomberg's mining-sector coverage.
flags.thesis_link: Consistent with the same crypto-price-exposure risk category flagged for HUT; MARA's own report was not re-read in full this run, treating as sector-consistent persistence of the 2026-06-30 flag.

flags.headline: STLD -3.37% — persisting AMBER, margin-compression thesis risk continues
flags.detail: Steel Dynamics fell 3.37% (price $221.68), continuing the decline from the 2026-06-30 guidance-miss flag. No new company-specific news found today.
flags.thesis_link: Matches STLD key_risk #1: 'profit margin compressed to 6.5% in 2025 despite record volumes.'

flags.headline: AEM flat (-0.15%) — downgraded RED→AMBER as gold stabilizes
flags.detail: Agnico Eagle is essentially flat today (price $154.92) as gold has stabilized around $4,068-4,075/oz, up modestly from the June 30 crash low near $4,040/oz. Gold remains ~16% below its ~$4,861 peak, so the underlying key_risk has not fully resolved, but the acute decline has paused.
flags.thesis_link: Matches AEM key_risk #1 (gold price reversion) — downgraded a tier as the price stabilizes rather than continuing to fall.

flags.headline: QCOM -1.52% — downgraded RED→AMBER as price stabilizes; structural memory-shortage risk unresolved
flags.detail: Qualcomm fell only 1.52% today, a marked improvement from June's -18% MTD decline. However, the underlying global memory/HBM shortage remains structurally unresolved — Micron's CEO said in June the shortage is expected to last through 2027, with memory-constrained manufacturers scaling back Qualcomm chipset orders.
flags.thesis_link: Structural risk, not in QCOM's screener key_risks list directly but consistent with prior RED thesis rationale on memory-driven order cuts; downgraded on stabilized price action, not resolved status.

flags.headline: NXPI -0.67% — resolved GREEN, automotive-chip softness no longer showing in price action
flags.detail: NXPI is essentially flat today after several weeks of automotive-sector softness. No fresh negative catalyst found.

flags.headline: DVN -2.54% (routine move) — guidance-suspension key_risk resolved
flags.detail: Devon Energy has formally issued combined 2026 production guidance (1.38 MMBoe/d, ~$4.9B capex, $1B annual pre-tax synergies) following the May 7, 2026 Coterra merger close. Today's -2.54% move is modest and appears energy-sector-driven rather than company-specific; the previously flagged guidance-visibility key_risk is resolved. Roster note: portfolios.json correctly lists DVN directly now (CTRA→DVN fix already applied).
flags.thesis_link: Resolves DVN key_risk #2: 'Coterra merger integration risk and the suspension of full-year guidance.' Guidance has been reinstated.

deal_watch.terms: $24.55 take-private (Permira / Warburg Pincus / Francisco Partners / Temasek)
deal_watch.note: Deal completed June 25 at $24.55/share, confirmed via Permira press release and multiple financial press sources. No new developments. CWAN remains delisted from NYSE; the outstanding action item is removing it from portfolios.json (tracked under roster_integrity).

levels.note: SPY at $745.665 (-0.13% today) is 15.46% above the $645.80 retracement support set 2026-04-13. QQQ -1.46% today ($724.65) — broad index weakness is far smaller than the semiconductor-specific rout, confirming today's selloff is sector-concentrated, not market-wide. No breach risk.

calendar.event: Q2 2026 earnings conference — hyperscaler/AI-capex tone check, quiet period begins July 6

Hyperscaler Q2 earnings window (MSFT confirmed July 29; AMZN/GOOG/META est. late July) — capex guidance remains the highest-priority macro signal for AI-infrastructure holdings; today's Meta-driven selloff shows the market is already pricing this anxiety in ahead of prints
Q2 2026 earnings (estimated, unconfirmed) — more than 2 weeks out
Q2 FY2027 earnings (estimated, unconfirmed) — more than 2 weeks out
Q2 FY2027 earnings (confirmed) — more than 2 weeks out
Q1 FY2027 earnings (estimated) — more than 2 weeks out
